# PocketBase v0.31.0 Go Migration with Seed Data

## Goal

Build a custom Go PocketBase v0.31.0 binary that ships exactly ONE reversible Go migration which creates two collections (`categories`, `articles`) and seeds them with deterministic initial data. The down step must remove both collections and the seeded records.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Binary path: /home/user/myproject/app
* Build command: `go build -o /home/user/myproject/app .` (executed in /home/user/myproject)
* Migrate up command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate up`
* Migrate down command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate down 1`
* Serve command: `/home/user/myproject/app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090`
* Port: 8090
* After `./app migrate up`:
  * `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 3` and the `items[].name` values are exactly the set {`Tech`, `Life`, `News`} (order not enforced).
  * `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 6` and every `items[].category` value equals the `id` of one of the three category records.
  * Each article record contains non-empty `title` and `body` string fields.
  * The `category` field on `articles` is a single relation to `categories`, required, with cascade delete behavior.
  * The `name` field on `categories` is unique.
* After `./app migrate down 1` (run once after a successful up):
  * Both `categories` and `articles` collections are absent: `GET /api/collections/categories/records` and `GET /api/collections/articles/records` both return HTTP 404.
* After running `./app migrate up` again following the down:
  * The same 3 categories (`Tech`, `Life`, `News`) and exactly 6 articles are restored deterministically.
I'll start by exploring the PocketBase v0.31.0 migration API and understanding the project structure needed.

todos.content: Set up Go module in /home/user/myproject with PocketBase v0.31.0 dependency
Create main.go with PocketBase app initialization
Create Go migration file with up/down steps (categories + articles collections, seed data)
Build the binary with `go build`
Verify migrate up, serve, and migrate down work correctly

# Extend with Go - Migrations - Docs - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-15, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/docs/go-migrations/)

Each migration file should have a single `m.Register(upFunc, downFunc)` call.
In the migration file, you are expected to write your "upgrade" code in the `upFunc` callback.
The `downFunc` is optional and it should contain the "downgrade" operations to revert the
changes made by the `upFunc`.
...
New unapplied migrations are automatically executed when the application server starts, aka. on
`serve`.
Alternatively, you can also apply new migrations manually by running `migrate up`.
To revert the last applied migration(s), you can run `migrate down [number]`.
When manually applying or reverting migrations, the `serve` process needs to be restarted so
that it can refresh its cached collections state.
...
`// migrations/1687801090_set_pending_status.go
package migrations
import (
"github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/core"
...
)
// set a default "pending" status to all empty status articles
func init() {
m.Register(func(app core.App) error {
_, err := app.DB().NewQuery("UPDATE articles SET status = 'pending' WHERE status = ''").Execute()
return err
}, nil)
}`

# How to do Migration when using pocket base as framework? #2218
created: 2024-06-19, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/2218)

Please check https://pocketbase.io/docs/migrations/#enable-go-migrations.
You don't have to deploy the `migrations` directory when using Go migrations, because they will be embedded in the final executable.
...
The example main.go file uses JS migrations because the migrations are external, aka.
not part of the executable.
If you are using PocketBase as framework you can use directly the Go API.
Once again, please make sure to check https://pocketbase.io/docs/migrations/#enable-go-migrations.
...
Please note that the `example/base/main.go` is used for the prebuilt executable.
You don't have to copy it 1:1.
The minimal PocketBase `main.go` could be found in https://pocketbase.io/docs/use-as-framework/.
If you still for some reason want to load JS migration for your custom binary, you have to enable also the `jsvm` migrations plugin as shown in https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/blob/master/examples/base/main.go#L70-L73.
But again, for most cases this shouldn't be necessary.
Please make sure to read https://pocketbase.io/docs/migrations/#enable-go-migrations.

# Documentation ¶
created: undefined, last updated: 2025-02-20, [Read more](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/tools/migrate)

func (l *MigrationsList) Register( up func(db dbx.Builder) error, down func(db dbx.Builder) error, optFilename ...string, )
Register adds new migration definition to the list.
If `optFilename` is not provided, it will try to get the name from its .go file.
The list will be sorted automatically based on the migrations file name.
...
NewRunner creates and initializes a new db migrations Runner instance.
#### func (*Runner) Down ¶
Down reverts the last `toRevertCount` applied migrations (in the order they were applied).
On success returns list with the reverted migrations file names.
...
Run interactively executes the current runner with the provided args.
The following commands are supported: - up - applies all migrations - down [n] - reverts the last n applied migrations

# Upgrade to v0.23.0 (Go) - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-09, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/v023upgrade/go/)

6. Upgrade the migration files (if any) to the new APIs.
If your existing `migrations` files
are all autogenerated, you may find it easier to upgrade by deleting all of them and run with the
v0.23.0 version of your app
`go run main.go migrate collections`
which will create a full snapshot of your latest collections configuration.
*Optionally, after that you can also run `go run migrate history-sync` to remove the deleted
migrations from the applied migrations history.*
7. Uncomment the `migrations` import statement.
8. Build your Go executable and test run your local changes (**including a test against no existing pb_data**).
9. If everything is working fine, create another more recent production backup for just in case.
After that deploy your app executable to the production server and restart the app service (e.g. `systemctl restart pocketbase`).
...
})

This allow access not only to the database but also to the app settings,

# Data migration flow · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #1604
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2025-06-16, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/1604)

You can create custom migrations in addition to the autogenerated ones.
They are executed in the order of the unapplied migration filenames (*each migration file is prefixed by default with a timestamp*).
For details how to write migrations in Go you can check https://pocketbase.io/docs/migrations/#migrate-create.
...
Here is a JS example flow:
1. Run
```
./pocketbase migrate create 'set_default_example_status'
```
2. Edit the previously created blank migration file:
```
...
Here is a JS example flow:
1. Run
```
./pocketbase migrate create 'set_default_example_status'
```
2. Edit the previously created blank migration file:
```
migrate((db) => {
// set a default "pending" status to all example records
db.newQuery("UPDATE example SET status = 'pending' WHERE status = ''").execute()
}, (db) => {
// no reverse...
})
```

# migratecmd package - github.com/pocketbase ...
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-24, [Read more](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/plugins/migratecmd)

Package migratecmd adds a new "migrate" command support to a PocketBase instance.
It also comes with automigrations support and templates generation
(both for JS and GO migration files).
Example usage:
```
migratecmd.MustRegister(app, app.RootCmd, migratecmd.Config{
TemplateLang: migratecmd.TemplateLangJS, // default to migratecmd.TemplateLangGo
Automigrate: true,
Dir: "/custom/migrations/dir", // optional template migrations path; default to "pb_migrations" (for JS) and "migrations" (for Go)
})
Note: To allow running JS migrations you'll need to enable first
[jsvm.MustRegister()].
```
...
func MustRegister(app core.App, rootCmd *cobra.Command, config Config)
```
MustRegister registers the migratecmd plugin to the provided app instance
and panic if it fails.
Example usage:
```
migratecmd.MustRegister(app, app.RootCmd, migratecmd.Config{})
```
...
```
Register registers the migratecmd plugin to the provided app instance.

# PocketBase迁移工具让数据库版本控制如丝般顺滑
created: 2025-10-03, last updated: 2026-03-24, [Read more](https://blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00830/article/details/151458756)

- Up：执行迁移的正向操作
- Down：回滚迁移的反向操作
- ReapplyCondition：控制迁移是否需要重新应用的条件判断
...
# 应用所有未执行的迁移
./pocketbase migrate up
# 回滚最近1个迁移（支持指定数量）
./pocketbase migrate down 1
# 同步迁移历史记录（移除已删除的迁移记录）
./pocketbase migrate history-sync
...
迁移工具的回滚功能采用**交互式确认** 设计，防止误操作。执行
```
down
```
命令时，系统会先列出待回滚的迁移文件，要求用户确认：
```
...
[y/N]
...
ReapplyCondition
```
方法，可实现智能迁移判断：
```
// 示例：仅当特定配置更改时重应用迁移
ReapplyCondition: func(txApp App, runner *MigrationsRunner, fileName string) (bool, error) {
settings, err := txApp.Settings().Get()
if err != nil {
return false, err
}
return settings.SmtpConfigured, nil
```

Name: "title",
Required: true,
Max: 100,
})
// add relation field
usersCollection, err := app.FindCollectionByNameOrId("users")
if err != nil {
return err
}
collection.Fields.Add(&core.RelationField{
Name: "user",
Required: true,
Max: 100,
CascadeDelete: true,
CollectionId: usersCollection.Id,
})
...
})

}
err = app.Delete(collection)

return err

# Interface RelationField
created: undefined, last updated: 2024-10-18, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/jsvm-rc/interfaces/core.RelationField.html)

ColumnType implements [Field.ColumnType] interface method.
...
CascadeDelete indicates whether the root model should be deleted in case of delete of all linked relations.
CollectionId is the id of the related collection.
MaxSelect indicates the max number of allowed relation records that could be linked to the main model.
For multiple select the value must be > 1, otherwise fallbacks to single (default).
If MinSelect is set, MaxSelect must be at least >= MinSelect.
MinSelect indicates the min number of allowed relation records that could be linked to the main model.
No min limit is applied if it is zero or negative value.
Required will require the field value to be non-empty.
...
RelationField defines "relation" type field for storing single or multiple collection record references.
Requires the CollectionId option to be set.
If MaxSelect is not set or <= 1, then the field value is expected to be a single record id.
If MaxSelect is > 1, then the field value is expected to be a slice of record ids.
The following additional setter keys are available:

# Class RelationField - PocketBase JSVM reference
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-03, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/jsvm/classes/RelationField.html)

RelationField defines "relation" type field for storing single or
multiple collection record references.
Requires the CollectionId option to be set.
If MaxSelect is not set or <= 1, then the field value is expected to be a single record id.
If MaxSelect is > 1, then the field value is expected to be a slice of record ids.
The respective zero record field value is either empty string (single) or empty string slice (multiple).
...
- "fieldName-" - subtract one or more values from the existing record one.
For example:
record.Set("categories-", "old1") // []string{"old2"}

# Relation field - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-07, [Read more](https://pocketbase-pocketbase.mintlify.app/fields/relation)

<ParamField path="collectionId" type="string" required>
The ID of the related collection.
This cannot be changed after the field is created.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="cascadeDelete" type="bool" default="false">
When true, deletes the current record if all its linked relations are deleted.
</ParamField>
...
The `collectionId` cannot be changed after the field is created to prevent breaking existing data relationships.
...
The field behavior changes based on `maxSelect`:
* `maxSelect <= 1`: Single relation mode (value is a record ID string)
* `maxSelect > 1`: Multiple relations mode (value is an array of record IDs)
...
</Tab>
<Tab title="With cascade delete">
```go theme={null}
field := &core.RelationField{
Name:          "project",
Required:      true,
MaxSelect:     1,
CollectionId:  "projects_collection_id",
CascadeDelete: true, // Delete task when project is deleted
}
…
...
## Zero value
* **Single relation**: Empty string `""`
* **Multiple relations**: Empty array `[]`

# Fields reference - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-28, [Read more](https://pocketbase-pocketbase.mintlify.app/collections/fields)

## Relation field
Links to records in other collections.
```go theme={null}
&core.RelationField{
Name:          "author",
Required:      true,
CollectionId:  "users_collection_id",
MaxSelect:     1,
MinSelect:     1,
CascadeDelete: false,
}
```
### Properties
* **CollectionId** - ID of the related collection (required)
* **MaxSelect** - Max related records (1 = single, >1 = multiple)
* **MinSelect** - Min required relations
* **CascadeDelete** - Delete record if all relations are deleted
* **Required** - Require at least one relation
<Warning>
**CollectionId cannot be changed** after the field is created.
You must delete and recreate the field to change the target collection.
</Warning>
...
```go theme={null}
// Append relations
record.Set("tags+", []string{"tag1_id", "tag2_id"})
// Prepend relations
record.Set("+tags", []string{"tag1_id"})
// Remove relations
record.Set("tags-", "tag1_id")
```

You can also enable the "Cascade delete" for the above 2 relation fields so that when a post or user is deleted to automatically delete the related

record(s).

part.text: Now let me create the main.go:

todos.content: Set up Go module in /home/user/myproject with PocketBase v0.31.0 dependency
Create main.go with PocketBase app initialization
Create Go migration file with up/down steps (categories + articles collections, seed data)
Build the binary with `go build`
Verify migrate up, serve, and migrate down work correctly

part.text:
Now let me create the migration file. This is the critical piece — it needs to:
1. Create `categories` collection with a unique `name` text field
2. Create `articles` collection with `title`, `body` text fields and a `category` relation field (single, required, cascade delete)
3. Seed 3 categories (Tech, Life, News) and 6 articles
4. Down: delete seeded records, then delete both collections
Now let's build:

todos.content: Set up Go module in /home/user/myproject with PocketBase v0.31.0 dependency
Create main.go with PocketBase app initialization
Create Go migration file with up/down steps (categories + articles collections, seed data)
Build the binary with `go build`
Verify migrate up, serve, and migrate down work correctly

part.text: Build succeeded. Let me verify the binary exists:

todos.content: Set up Go module in /home/user/myproject with PocketBase v0.31.0 dependency
Create main.go with PocketBase app initialization
Create Go migration file with up/down steps (categories + articles collections, seed data)
Build the binary with `go build`
Verify migrate up, serve, and migrate down work correctly

part.text: Now let's test. First, clean any existing pb_data:
Run migrate up:
Migration applied successfully. Now let's start the server in the background and test the API:
Need to set the list rules to allow public access. The collections need `ListRule` and `ViewRule` set to empty (which means no auth required). Let me update the migration:
I need to add `ListRule` and `ViewRule` to allow public access. I also need to import `types` for the pointer helper. Let me update the migration:

input.replaceContent:
// ── Create categories collection ──
		categories := core.NewBaseCollection("categories")
		categories.ListRule = types.Pointer("")
		categories.ViewRule = types.Pointer("")
		categories.Fields.Add(

// ── Create articles collection ──
		articles := core.NewBaseCollection("articles")
		articles.ListRule = types.Pointer("")
		articles.ViewRule = types.Pointer("")
		articles.Fields.Add(

part.text: Now kill the server, clean up, rebuild, and test again:
Still getting 403. The issue is that PocketBase by default restricts API access. Let me check the server output for clues:

output.output:
Error: listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8090: bind: address already in use

Process exited with code 0

part.text: Port is still in use from the previous instance. Let me kill anything on 8090 and restart:

output.output:
done
/bin/bash: line 1: lsof: command not found

port 8090 is free
/bin/bash: line 1: ss: command not found

part.text: Good, port is free now. Let me restart:
The issue might be that PocketBase caches the collection schema from the migration but the serve process started before the migration was applied. Let me check the server logs:

output.output:
Error: listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8090: bind: address already in use

Process exited with code 0

part.text: Something else is holding port 8090. Let me check more carefully:
Wait, the server IS responding on port 8090, but the background job says it failed to bind. That means the old process from the first serve attempt is still running. Let me find and kill it:

input.command: ps aux | grep "app serve" | grep -v grep 2>&1

output.output:
root         251  0.0  0.0   4324  3536 ?        Ss   04:01   0:00 bash -c set -o pipefail; pochi --model deepseek/deepseek-4-pro --max-steps 200 --max-retries 10 --blobs-dir /logs/agent/pochi/blobs --experimental-stream-trajectory /logs/agent/pochi/trajectory.jsonl > >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stdout.txt) 2> >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stderr.txt >&2) <<'EOF' # PocketBase v0.31.0 Go Migration with Seed Data  ## Goal Build a custom Go PocketBase v0.31.0 binary that ships exactly ONE reversible Go migration which creates two collections (`categories`, `articles`) and seeds them with deterministic initial data. The down step must remove both collections and the seeded records.  ## Acceptance Criteria - Project path: /home/user/myproject - Binary path: /home/user/myproject/app - Build command: `go build -o /home/user/myproject/app .` (executed in /home/user/myproject) - Migrate up command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate up` - Migrate down command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate down 1` - Serve command: `/home/user/myproject/app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090` - Port: 8090 - After `./app migrate up`:   - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 3` and the `items[].name` values are exactly the set {`Tech`, `Life`, `News`} (order not enforced).   - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 6` and every `items[].category` value equals the `id` of one of the three category records.   - Each article record contains non-empty `title` and `body` string fields.   - The `category` field on `articles` is a single relation to `categories`, required, with cascade delete behavior.   - The `name` field on `categories` is unique. - After `./app migrate down 1` (run once after a successful up):   - Both `categories` and `articles` collections are absent: `GET /api/collections/categories/records` and `GET /api/collections/articles/records` both return HTTP 404. - After running `./app migrate up` again following the down:   - The same 3 categories (`Tech`, `Life`, `News`) and exactly 6 articles are restored deterministically.   EOF
root        3816  0.0  0.0 1991404 24568 ?       Sl   04:06   0:00 ./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090

part.text: There it is — PID 3816. Let me kill it:

input.command: kill -9 3816 2>&1; sleep 1; ps aux | grep "app serve" | grep -v grep || echo "process killed"

output.output: root         251  0.0  0.0   4324  3536 ?        Ss   04:01   0:00 bash -c set -o pipefail; pochi --model deepseek/deepseek-4-pro --max-steps 200 --max-retries 10 --blobs-dir /logs/agent/pochi/blobs --experimental-stream-trajectory /logs/agent/pochi/trajectory.jsonl > >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stdout.txt) 2> >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stderr.txt >&2) <<'EOF' # PocketBase v0.31.0 Go Migration with Seed Data  ## Goal Build a custom Go PocketBase v0.31.0 binary that ships exactly ONE reversible Go migration which creates two collections (`categories`, `articles`) and seeds them with deterministic initial data. The down step must remove both collections and the seeded records.  ## Acceptance Criteria - Project path: /home/user/myproject - Binary path: /home/user/myproject/app - Build command: `go build -o /home/user/myproject/app .` (executed in /home/user/myproject) - Migrate up command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate up` - Migrate down command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate down 1` - Serve command: `/home/user/myproject/app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090` - Port: 8090 - After `./app migrate up`:   - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 3` and the `items[].name` values are exactly the set {`Tech`, `Life`, `News`} (order not enforced).   - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 6` and every `items[].category` value equals the `id` of one of the three category records.   - Each article record contains non-empty `title` and `body` string fields.   - The `category` field on `articles` is a single relation to `categories`, required, with cascade delete behavior.   - The `name` field on `categories` is unique. - After `./app migrate down 1` (run once after a successful up):   - Both `categories` and `articles` collections are absent: `GET /api/collections/categories/records` and `GET /api/collections/articles/records` both return HTTP 404. - After running `./app migrate up` again following the down:   - The same 3 categories (`Tech`, `Life`, `News`) and exactly 6 articles are restored deterministically.   EOF

part.text: Good, killed. Now, the issue with the 403 error on the old process is that it was started before the migration was applied. The `serve` process auto-applies migrations on startup, but the first `serve` was started with the old binary (before the ListRule/ViewRule changes). Let me now start fresh with a clean pb_data and the updated binary:
Categories API returns 200 with `totalItems: 3` and the names are `Tech`, `Life`, `News`. Now let's test the articles:

input.command:
curl -s http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
print('totalItems:', data['totalItems'])
cat_ids = set()
for item in data['items']:
    print(f\"  id={item['id']}, title={item['title'][:50]}..., category={item['category']}\")
    cat_ids.add(item['category'])
    assert item['title'], 'title is empty'
    assert item['body'], 'body is empty'
print('Unique category IDs:', cat_ids)
print('All checks passed!')
"

part.text: All 6 articles, each with non-empty title and body, and each category ID maps to one of the three category records. Now let's verify that the category IDs match:

input.command:
curl -s http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for item in data['items']:
    print(f\"  name={item['name']}, id={item['id']}\")
"

output.output:
name=Tech, id=2fme5s1i1lhwdmf
  name=Life, id=8xg544fdkdbsqla
  name=News, id=i31dxz8u5uz7w7c
